chapters.title: The Last Lesson by Alphonse Daudet
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Loss of language equals loss of identity. The chapter highlights patriotism, regret, and the importance of mother tongue.

chapters.title: Lost Spring by Anees Jung
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Child labour, poverty, exploitation, and the vicious cycle of tradition that kills dreams.

chapters.title: Deep Water by William Douglas
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Conquering fear through determination, willpower, and systematic effort. The lesson contrasts fear of death with fear itself.

chapters.title: The Rattrap by Selma Lagerlöf
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Human goodness can reform a person. Compassion and dignity bring out the best in people.

chapters.title: Indigo by Louis Fischer
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: First instance of Satyagraha in India. The chapter highlights self-reliance, courage, non-violence, and leadership that empowers the poor.

chapters.title: Poets and Pancakes by Asokamitran
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Humor and irony in the film world. The chapter shows the gap between art and commercial cinema and between bureaucracy and creativity.

chapters.title: The Interview by Christopher Silvester / Umberto Eco
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: The chapter explores the value and danger of interviews, the ethics of public questioning, and Umberto Eco’s discipline in using small moments of time.

chapters.title: Going Places by A. R. Barton
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Fantasy versus reality, adolescent dreams, class barriers, and escapism.

chapters.title: My Mother at Sixty-six by Kamala Das
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Ageing, separation, fear of loss, and the bond between mother and daughter. The poem shows how we hide pain behind smiles when facing mortality.

chapters.title: Keeping Quiet by Pablo Neruda
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Need for introspection, peace, and universal brotherhood. Silence helps us understand ourselves and stop harming nature and each other.

chapters.title: A Thing of Beauty by John Keats
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Beauty is eternal and gives us hope. In a world full of pain and evil, beauty in nature and art heals us and gives meaning to life.

chapters.title: A Roadside Stand by Robert Frost
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Economic inequality, exploitation of the rural poor, false promises of government, and the city-village divide.

chapters.title: Aunt Jennifer's Tigers by Adrienne Rich
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Male domination in marriage, suppression of women, and art as an escape. The tigers show Aunt Jennifer’s inner desire for freedom.

chapters.title: The Third Level by Jack Finney
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Escapism, nostalgia for a simpler past, and the harshness of modern life. The third level is a metaphor for the human desire to escape stress and insecurity.

chapters.title: The Tiger King by Kalki
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Abuse of power, pride, fate, and satire on rulers who misuse power for vanity. The story also suggests that harming nature backfires.

chapters.title: Journey to the End of the Earth by Tishani Doshi
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Climate change, environmental responsibility, Earth’s history, and the need for global cooperation.

chapters.title: The Enemy by Pearl S. Buck
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Conflict between human compassion and national loyalty, duty as a doctor versus duty as a citizen, and humanity rising above hatred.

chapters.title: On the Face of It by Susan Hill
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Disability, loneliness, self-acceptance, empathy, and the importance of inner worth over outward appearance.

chapters.title: Memories of Childhood by Zitkala-Sa and Bama
chapters.subject: English
chapters.description: Racial discrimination, caste discrimination, cultural suppression, humiliation, resistance, and the power of education.

chapters.title: Electrochemistry
chapters.subject: Chemistry
chapters.description: Electrochemistry is the branch of chemistry that deals with the relationship between chemical energy and electrical energy. It explains how chemical reactions can produce electricity and how electricity can bring about che...

chapters.title: Chemical Kinetics
chapters.subject: Chemistry
chapters.description: Chemical kinetics is the branch of chemistry that deals with the speed of chemical reactions and the factors affecting the speed. It explains how fast a reaction happens, why some reactions are fast or slow, and how the sp...

chapters.title: Coordination Compounds
chapters.subject: Chemistry
chapters.description: A coordination compound is a compound in which a central metal atom or ion is surrounded by molecules or ions called ligands. These ligands are attached to the central metal by coordinate bonds. The metal ion acts as the c...

chapters.title: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes
chapters.subject: Chemistry
chapters.description: Haloalkanes and haloarenes are organic compounds formed when one or more hydrogen atoms in hydrocarbons are replaced by halogen atoms such as F, Cl, Br or I.

chapters.title: Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers
chapters.subject: Chemistry
chapters.description: This chapter deals with organic compounds containing oxygen. Alcohols, phenols and ethers are important classes of organic compounds.

chapters.title: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids
chapters.subject: Chemistry
chapters.description: This chapter deals with organic compounds containing the carbonyl group, >C=O. Aldehydes and ketones are called carbonyl compounds. Carboxylic acids also contain a carbonyl group, but they have an additional hydroxyl group...

chapters.title: Amines
chapters.subject: Chemistry
chapters.description: Amines are organic compounds derived from ammonia, NH3. In ammonia, nitrogen is bonded to three hydrogen atoms. When one or more hydrogen atoms of ammonia are replaced by alkyl groups or aryl groups, the compounds formed a...

chapters.title: Biomolecules
chapters.subject: Chemistry
chapters.description: Biomolecules are naturally occurring chemical substances present in living organisms. They are essential for life processes such as growth, energy production, repair, protection, reproduction, heredity and regulation of bo...

chapters.title: Current Electricity
chapters.subject: Physics
chapters.description: Electric current means the flow of electric charge through a conductor. In a metal wire, the charges that move are free electrons. When a battery is connected, these electrons drift through the wire and current is produced...

chapters.title: Moving Charges and Magnetism
chapters.subject: Physics
chapters.description: Moving electric charges produce magnetic fields. A charge at rest produces only an electric field, while a moving charge produces both an electric field and a magnetic field. Since electric current is the flow of charges,...

chapters.title: Electromagnetic Induction
chapters.subject: Physics
chapters.description: Electromagnetic induction is the phenomenon of producing an electric current or emf in a coil or circuit by changing the magnetic field or magnetic flux linked with it. Electricity can produce magnetism, and changing magne...

chapters.title: Electromagnetic Waves
chapters.subject: Physics
chapters.description: Ampere’s circuital law in its original form was incomplete for situations where electric field changes with time, especially during the charging of a capacitor.

chapters.title: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
chapters.subject: Physics
chapters.description: Ray optics, also called geometrical optics, is the branch of optics in which light is treated as travelling in straight lines called rays. It studies how light travels, reflects, refracts, forms images, and works in optica...

chapters.title: Atoms
chapters.subject: Physics
chapters.description: After the discovery of the electron, J.J. Thomson proposed a model to explain how positive and negative charges are arranged inside an atom. According to Thomson, an atom is a positively charged sphere in which electrons a...

chapters.title: Nuclei
chapters.subject: Physics
chapters.description: An atom has a very small central part called the nucleus. Almost the entire mass of the atom is concentrated in the nucleus. The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Protons and neutrons together are called nucleons.
